visualGoal: Make a marine simmer buy the SIM first and the RIGHT axis gear second, by proving the compatibility gate with verified product photos of the sims-as-context tiles, the glue-candidate axis controllers, the digital button boxes, and the DIY boards. No procedural art. Every embedded image is a verified marine product photo; the two primary repurposed axis controllers (G29 helm, Saitek quadrant) stay text-only because no verified marine-context photo exists.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The single most-recommended helm stand-in in the bay, but currently text + Amazon link only because there is no verified marine-context photo. Needs a real, verified photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid tile or before certification if the shortlist should show the primary axis controllers.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Saitek Pro Flight Throttle Quadrant (as engine telegraph)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Best value in the bay and the second primary axis controller; text-only until a real verified photo is sourced. Same certification gate as the G29.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Dim ship bridge console at dusk, glowing teal radar and chart displays, brushed-metal helm station with an empty mounting shelf left open for a real product overlay, a subtle split-lighting motif (green 'reads your axes' side vs red 'keyboard-only' side), curator Gus Calder studying the console, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI text, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake wheelbase, warped controls, tiny unreadable UI text, procedural gradient art, invented sim screenshots

infographicPrompts.title: Does this sim read your gear?
content: Does the store page list analog-axis / controller support?
NO / unofficial (Ships At Sea, Seafarer) -> button box + glue software, don't buy a wheel yet
ALWAYS -> re-verify on the current build
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center flow, large readable text, teal/amber marine palette, verified sim tiles as node markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: 2026 marine sim compatibility map
content: Professional Ship Simulator: promised (verify at launch)
infographicPrompts.style: Row-per-sim table, green/amber/red status chips, verified product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult marine-sim curator Gus Calder at a home bridge desk, one hand on a repurposed throttle lever, checking a sim's store page on a monitor, calm methodical energy, practical not fantasy, product overlay space beside him, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.item: Bridge Command in-sim controls/settings screen (native axis assignment)
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Only if captured first-party or used under fair-use editorial with attribution; do NOT fabricate a screenshot. Purely optional supporting media, not required for the article.
Same rule: real capture or skip. Article stands on verified product photos without it.

openQuestions: Source verified marine-context photos for the Logitech G29 (helm) and Saitek Pro Flight Throttle Quadrant so the two primary axis controllers can become body visuals?
Re-verify current-build controller status for Seafarer, Fishing: Barents Sea, and Ships At Sea (cited threads are 2018-2024) before certification.
Confirm the shipped Professional Ship Simulator input spec (exact axis count, rudder-deadzone option) at Early Access launch; the 'multiple axes / 100+ buttons' claim is currently promise-level.
